decontamination_notes: Original scenario written for VulcanBench on 2026-06-28. The ApiClient house style, the list_orders task, and the mergeability rubric are not derived from any existing repository, so they post-date all model training cutoffs.

blocking: list_orders fetches GET /users/{user_id}/orders and respects the page argument (page 2 must request page=2), returning the parsed orders.
The change modifies only apiclient/client.py and adds no new files and no new third-party dependency.

weighted.criterion: The HTTP call goes through self._request(...) so it inherits the client's auth header and HTTPError->ApiError handling, rather than calling urllib/requests directly.
Failures surface as ApiError consistent with the other methods, rather than being swallowed (e.g. returning None) or raising a different exception type.
Response parsing reuses self._parse(...) rather than calling json.loads or handling .read() directly.
The method signature, docstring, and concise one-line body style match the neighboring get_user/get_order methods.
